title: Review this uncommitted diff for bugs, security issues, and code quality. Be concise (max 20 lines).
summary_text: 1. High: [auto-push-after-commit.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/auto-push-after-commit.sh#L27) decides commit success by scanning `stdout` text, then [unconditionally pushes](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/auto-push-after-commit.sh#L35). A failed `git commit` commonly reports on `stderr`, so this can publish previously local-only commits by accident. 2. High: [note-thumbnail-gen.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/note-generate/scripts/note-thumbnail-gen.js#L462) runs Gate 2, retries once on failure, but never re-runs Gate 2 on the retried image and still reports the original gate result at [line 486](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/note-generate/scripts/note-thumbnail-gen.js#L486). The saved file and reported quality status can diverge. 3. Medium: [thumbnail-gate2.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/note-generate/scripts/thumbnail-gate2.js#L92) always sends `mimeType: 'image/png'`, but Manus explicitly accepts/returns JPEG too in [note-thumbnail-manus.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/note-generate/scripts/note-thumbnail-manus.js#L117). JPEG outputs will be misclassified, likely causing Gate 2 to fail open and silently skip validation. 4. Medium: [canon-build-debug.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-build-debug.sh#L11) tracks a single global `LAST_LOG_SIZE` for all files, then reuses it inside the per-file loop at [lines 58-72](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-build-debug.sh#L58). Once one file grows, offsets for the others are wrong, so error monitoring will miss or duplicate log lines. 5. Medium: Shell-mode task execution in [canon-build-cicd.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-build-cicd.sh#L118) and [canon-build-debug.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-build-debug.sh#L102) uses `eval "$task" ... || true`, then reads `$?`. That guarantees `EXIT_CODE=0` for failing shell tasks, so the failure counter and error reporting are broken.
